topics.title: Extreme heat disrupts holiday plans
topics.summary: A major heat wave is disrupting Fourth of July events across the U.S., straining power grids and forcing organizers to adjust outdoor celebrations.
topics.discussionPrompt: Big summer events sound fun until the weather turns risky. Would you still go, or would you rather celebrate somewhere cool and quiet?

topics.title: Global temperatures run unusually hot
topics.summary: Reuters reports global temperatures are running well above normal, with intense heat affecting parts of the Northern Hemisphere during early July.
topics.discussionPrompt: It feels like extreme heat is becoming part of ordinary summer planning. How should cities and events adapt without ruining everything fun?

topics.title: World Cup faces abuse concerns
topics.summary: The global players union FIFPRO condemned racist abuse aimed at footballers during the 2026 World Cup and called for stronger action from authorities.
topics.discussionPrompt: Sports can bring people together, but bad fan behaviour can spoil it fast. What should leagues do when abuse follows players online and in stadiums?

topics.title: Colombia advances at the World Cup
topics.summary: Colombia beat Ghana 1-0 in Kansas City, staying unbeaten and setting up a last-16 World Cup match against Switzerland in Vancouver.
topics.discussionPrompt: A single goal can completely change a tournament story. Do you enjoy low-scoring tense games, or do you prefer wild, high-scoring matches?

topics.title: Rare Declaration copy found
topics.summary: A rare early copy of the U.S. Declaration of Independence was found in the UK National Archives after being overlooked among old naval records.
topics.discussionPrompt: It is amazing that important history can hide in plain sight. What forgotten object would you most like to discover in an archive?
